expectations.text: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: model create model_name = crane, followed by part create rigid_body name_and_position for .crane.tower, .crane.boom, and .crane.payload

expectations.text: Boom markers use eval() with loc_global() to compute positions relative to the boom's CM
mkr_boom_tip uses ori_along_axis() for orientation toward the payload
Every eval expression (eval(...)) stays entirely on one line - no & inside parentheses
expectations.evidence: All three eval expressions (two loc_global, one ori_along_axis) are each on a single line with no & continuation inside the parentheses. A comment in the script even cites Rule 16.

expectations.text: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines (comma-separated quoted strings), not on one enormous line
expectations.evidence: function = 'STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)', & ' + STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)', & ' - 2.0 * VZ(...)' - correctly split as comma-separated quoted strings

expectations.text: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom)
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute for jnt_tower_ground and jnt_boom_tower; constraint create joint spherical for jnt_payload_boom

expectations.text: Force function includes STEP5 ramps and a VZ velocity-damping term
Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: All three parts use part modify rigid_body mass_properties with center_of_mass_marker referencing pre-created .cm markers

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names use full .crane.* paths throughout the script
No adams_id keyword appears anywhere in the script

expectations.text: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder for .crane.tower.cyl_body and .crane.boom.cyl_body; geometry create shape ellipsoid for .crane.payload.ellip_body

expectations.text: Uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient & type = auto_select & end_time = 3.0 & number_of_steps = 3000 & model_name = .crane & initial_static = no
